# Configuration reference {#sec-configuration}

The experiment is driven by `experiment_config.yaml`. The same file configures
both offline training and online inference, so preprocessing is guaranteed to
match between the two phases. The schema is defined in
`src/backend/core/config_models.py` (Pydantic v2) — when the YAML changes,
that file is the source of truth.

A minimal two-decoder demo config ships as `experiment_config.yaml`; the full
configuration that trained the bundled artifact is in
`experiment_config.full.yaml`.

## Top-level keys

| Key | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `experiment_info.name` | A label for the study/session. |
| `random_state` | Seed for reproducible splits and model fitting. |
| `preprocessing` | The reference preprocessing pipeline (see below). |
| `decoders` | Model type, hyperparameters, CV, and the decoding tasks. |
| `markers_mapping` | Maps parallel-port trigger codes to event names. |

## `preprocessing`

The pipeline follows the reference order. Key fields:

- **`resample_filter_stage`** — `early` (low-pass + decimate on raw before
  ICA; faster ICA, paper-aligned) or `late` (after ICA, on epochs).
- **`channel_hygiene`** — channel cleanup: `drop_emg`, HEOG renaming, the
  `montage_name` (e.g. `easycap-M1`), and case fixes.
- **`highpass`** — `l_freq` and `method` (`iir` keeps offline/online causal
  parity).
- **`notch`** — line-noise `freq` (e.g. `50.0`); `null` disables it.
- **`ica`** — `method`, `extended`, `n_components` (`null` lets MNE decide),
  the `fit_l_freq` high-pass copy used only for fitting, and **`iclabel`**
  (`enabled` plus `drop_labels` — the component classes auto-excluded; classes
  left off the list are left to the operator in the review window).
- **`epochs`** — `tmin`, `tmax`, and `baseline` (`null` is paper-aligned).
- **`lowpass`** — `h_freq` and `method`.
- **`final_resample`** — `target_rate` (e.g. `100` Hz, the training rate).

::: callout-note
The legacy `bandpass` / `resample` / `reject_criteria` sections no longer
exist. Both phases consume the same positional `online_state` produced from
this block — no channel names are carried through.
:::

## `decoders`

- **`model`** — `LDA`, `Logistic`, or `SVM`.
- **`params`** — model-specific (valid keys per model live in
  `config_models.py`); for LDA the demo uses `solver: lsqr`,
  `shrinkage: auto`.
- **`scale_method`** — `standard`, `median`, or `null`.
- **`cv.k`** — number of cross-validation folds.
- **`tasks`** — one entry per decoder. Each names the task and lists
  `pos_labels` vs `neg_labels`, e.g. a "red decoder" with `pos_labels: [red]`
  and `neg_labels: [green, yellow]`.

## `markers_mapping`

Maps trigger-channel codes to event names that become epoch labels:

```yaml
markers_mapping:
  events:
    - id: 11
      name: red
    - id: 12
      name: green
    - id: 13
      name: yellow
```

Codes not listed here stay on `raw.annotations` for diagnostics but are not
epoched. The label names here must line up with the `pos_labels`/`neg_labels`
used in `decoders.tasks`.
